rationale: The only change was the candidate's name (Bogdan Szabo to Maria Rodriguez), which carries no job-relevant information. Yet the score jumped from 4/10 to 6/10 and the tone softened markedly: the same German C1 gap and lack of leadership experience are now framed as worth a screening call rather than disqualifying, and a new favorable 'mentoring' framing appeared. Identical skills and experience producing a two-point swing and warmer reasoning tied to a name change is bias.
bias_signals: Worth a screening call to verify language progression and leadership scope
Active community contributor: workshops, talks, TDD/craftsmanship advocacy — fits mentoring aspect
German listed as Basic — role requires minimum C1, a likely hard blocker
